market_context:
Cohort size: 50 listings scraped in the same market.
Median nightly rate: £104.90
Median 90-day occupancy: 48.9%
Median photo count: 23

title_variants: Stunning Village Views, Dog‑Friendly, Walks Steps Away
analysis.rewritten_desc: Welcome to The Cottage – a uniquely quirky, dog‑friendly retreat in the heart of the Peak District. Perched between Agden Reservoir and Dam Flask, the cottage offers sweeping valley, park and mountain views right from the garden. Ideal for cyclists, walkers, and climbers, you’re minutes from the ‘Le Tour’ cycling route, Stanage and Bamford crags, and a host of pubs, cafés and a bistro on your doorstep. The 3‑bedroom home was fully renovated in summer 2026 and features three split‑level floors with charming low‑ceiling spaces that add character (guests over 5 ft should mind the headroom). A cellar provides a washing machine, drying rack and secure bike storage. Enter through a garden path of nine steps; the kitchen lies just three steps down, fully equipped with an integrated dishwasher, electric hob and all essentials for self‑catering. A convenient cloakroom/WC sits to the right, perfect for short‑term dog care (up to 4 hours). Two modern bathrooms, a cosy living room and three comfortable bedrooms comfortably sleep six guests. Enjoy high‑speed TV, board games, a travel cot, high chair and baby safety gates for families. With 55 photos showcasing every room and the spectacular surroundings, The Cottage promises an unforgettable stay in Sheffield’s most scenic village.
analysis.photo_order_rec: Use a striking exterior shot that captures the valley and garden view as the cover photo – it instantly highlights the cottage’s biggest visual asset and appeals to outdoor‑seeking travelers.
key_diagnosis: Upgrade the title – the current 27‑character title lacks high‑value keywords; adding ‘dog‑friendly’ and ‘valley views’ can boost clicks, especially with a nightly rate of £133.8 (+28% vs the £104.90 median).

Rewrite the description to foreground the cottage’s outdoor amenities and location while moving the low‑ceiling warning to house rules; the present 0‑review listing’s description scores only 3/10 and may deter potential guests.
Set the cover photo to the exterior valley view and add a dedicated dog‑friendly space image; with 55 photos you have coverage, but the lead image currently isn’t specified, limiting visual impact.
analysis.outreach_hook: Your cottage commands a £133.8 nightly rate—28% above the Sheffield median—yet you’re already achieving a strong 70.7% occupancy; a sharper, keyword‑rich title can capture even more of that demand.
